title: Recover the Min Heap After Deletion of Duplicate Elements

statement.markdown: Given an array nums, remove duplicate elements while preserving the min-heap property. Note that you can't remove an element from the middle of the array, so you'll have to shift all elements to the right of the element to be removed first. Then, heapify the array from the root down to the leaf nodes.
statement.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer n, representing the number of elements in the array. The second line contains n space-separated integers representing the elements in the array.
statement.notes: n will be at least 1 and at most 10000.

hints.text: Make sure to shift all elements to the right of the element to be removed first.

editorial.title: Solution Explanation
editorial.intuition: The idea is to remove duplicates and then heapify the array
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force solution involves removing duplicates and then applying heapify from the root down to the leaf nodes
editorial.betterSolution: Better solution can be achieved by using a set to store unique elements and then applying heapify
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution involves using a set to store unique elements and then applying heapify
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness lies in the fact that we are preserving the min-heap property
editorial.dryRun: Dry run can be performed by printing the array after each operation
commonMistakes: Removing an element from the middle of the array
